{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "|新撰姓氏録" to "|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oomizuguchi no {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}}== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}}== See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大田田根子" to "|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]]
