revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] {{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神|12=draft}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=draft}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|12=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|12=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=draft}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=draft}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=draft}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=draft}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=draft}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神|12=draft}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=draft}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|12=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

revision.comment: /* 氏族 */ 金吾龍神社宮司についての記述を追加。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大水口宿禰命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大水口宿禰、大水口命、大水口足尼 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[孝霊天皇]]、[[崇神天皇]]、[[垂仁天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[出石心大臣命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>？、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]？、母：新河小楯姫<ref name="tenson"/>？ | 兄弟 = [[大矢口宿禰命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[建忍山垂根]]？ | 特記事項 = }} '''大水口宿禰'''（おおみなくちのすくね{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}/おおみくちのすくね）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の遠祖とされる。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京神別 天神 穂積臣」条では、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]（伊我男命）と新河小楯姫命の子とも、[[ニギハヤヒ|神饒速日命]]の六世孫とも伝える。
一方、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天孫本紀」や「物部」系図では[[出石心大臣命]]（いずしこころのおおおみのみこと、饒速日尊三世孫）と新河小楯姫の子と記され、系譜に異同がある{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}<ref name="keifu4"/>。なお、同書では伊香色雄命（伊香賀色雄）は饒速日尊六世孫とされる。
子に関して史書では明らかでない。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]7年8月7日条によると、大水口宿禰は倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫（[[倭迹迹日百襲姫命]]）・[[伊勢麻績君]]（いせのおみのきみ）とともに同じ夢を見て、[[大物主|大物主神]]（のちの[[大神神社]]祭神）と[[倭大国魂神]]（のちの[[大和神社]]祭神）の祭主をそれぞれ[[大田田根子|大田田根子命]]と[[市磯長尾市]]にするよう告げられた旨を天皇に奏上した{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
また、同書[[垂仁天皇]]25年3月条では「一云」の中で、倭大神が大水口宿禰に神憑り、倭大神を祀ることを告げたことが記されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『日本書紀』では、大水口宿禰について[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]遠祖としている。
また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京神別 天神 [[穂積氏|穂積臣]] - 伊香賀色雄男の大水口宿禰の後。 * 右京神別 天神 [[采女氏|采女朝臣]] - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後。
『先代旧事本紀』「天孫本紀」でも、同様に穂積臣・采女臣の祖である旨が記載されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
[[金吾龍神社]]の宮司が大水口宿禰の後裔を名乗っている<ref>{{Webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925133249/https://kingoryujin.org/about/ |date=2020年9月25日}}</ref>。
=== 国造 === 『先代旧事本紀』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[末羅国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に穂積臣同祖の大水口足尼の孫の[[矢田稲吉命|矢田稲吉]]を国造に定める。のちの[[肥前国]][[松浦郡]]周辺にあたる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <ref name="keifu4">[[中田憲信]]「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> }}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大水口宿禰|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[大和神社]] （[[奈良県]][[天理市]]） * [[水口神社 (甲賀市)|水口神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]） * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみなくちのすくね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京神別 天神 [[穂積氏|穂積臣]] - 伊香賀色雄男の大水口宿禰の後。 * 右京神別 天神 [[采女氏|采女朝臣]] - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後。
『先代旧事本紀』「天孫本紀」でも、同様に穂積臣・采女臣の祖である旨が記載されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
[[金吾龍神社]]の宮司が大水口宿禰の後裔（88世大水口宿禰）を名乗っている。金吾龍神社との関わりは16世紀に遡るとしているほか<ref>{{Webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925133249/https://kingoryujin.org/about/ |date=2020年9月25日}}</ref>、代々の大水口宿禰は、中世に消滅しかけた[[アラハバキ]]信仰の庇護者であるとしている<ref>{{Cite Web |url= https://kingoryujin.org/arahabaki/|title= アラハバキ神社／あらはばき神社｜東京・代々木・南新宿|publisher= 金吾龍神社|accessdate=2025-04-13}}</ref>。
=== 国造 === 『先代旧事本紀』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[末羅国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に穂積臣同祖の大水口足尼の孫の[[矢田稲吉命|矢田稲吉]]を国造に定める。のちの[[肥前国]][[松浦郡]]周辺にあたる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <ref name="keifu4">[[中田憲信]]「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> }}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大水口宿禰|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[大和神社]] （[[奈良県]][[天理市]]） * [[水口神社 (甲賀市)|水口神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]） * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみなくちのすくね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大水口宿禰命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大水口宿禰、大水口命、大水口足尼 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[孝霊天皇]]、[[崇神天皇]]、[[垂仁天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[出石心大臣命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>？、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]？、母：新河小楯姫<ref name="tenson"/>？ | 兄弟 = [[大矢口宿禰命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[建忍山垂根]]？ | 特記事項 = }} '''大水口宿禰'''（おおみなくちのすくね{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}/おおみくちのすくね）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の遠祖とされる。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京神別 天神 穂積臣」条では、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]（伊我男命）と新河小楯姫命の子とも、[[ニギハヤヒ|神饒速日命]]の六世孫とも伝える。
一方、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天孫本紀」や「物部」系図では[[出石心大臣命]]（いずしこころのおおおみのみこと、饒速日尊三世孫）と新河小楯姫の子と記され、系譜に異同がある{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}<ref name="keifu4"/>。なお、同書では伊香色雄命（伊香賀色雄）は饒速日尊六世孫とされる。
子に関して史書では明らかでない。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]7年8月7日条によると、大水口宿禰は倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫（[[倭迹迹日百襲姫命]]）・[[伊勢麻績君]]（いせのおみのきみ）とともに同じ夢を見て、[[大物主|大物主神]]（のちの[[大神神社]]祭神）と[[倭大国魂神]]（のちの[[大和神社]]祭神）の祭主をそれぞれ[[大田田根子|大田田根子命]]と[[市磯長尾市]]にするよう告げられた旨を天皇に奏上した{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
また、同書[[垂仁天皇]]25年3月条では「一云」の中で、倭大神が大水口宿禰に神憑り、倭大神を祀ることを告げたことが記されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『日本書紀』では、大水口宿禰について[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]遠祖としている。
また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京神別 天神 [[穂積氏|穂積臣]] - 伊香賀色雄男の大水口宿禰の後。 * 右京神別 天神 [[采女氏|采女朝臣]] - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後。
『先代旧事本紀』「天孫本紀」でも、同様に穂積臣・采女臣の祖である旨が記載されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
2013年より[[金吾龍神社]]の宮司を務める[[クリスチャントゥデイ#日本オリベットアッセンブリー教団（旧あいのひかり教団）に関する動向|水口結貴]]<ref>{{Cite Web|url=https://yuhobika.net/yuhobikaweb/kingoryu-jinja/|title=【金吾龍神社】古代祭祀の中心地に鎮座し 縄文信仰を今に伝える龍神神社 | ゆほびかweb|website=ゆほびかweb|publisher=マキノ出版|accessdate=2025-04-14}}</ref>が、大水口宿禰の後裔（88世大水口宿禰）を名乗っている。大水口宿禰と金吾龍神社との関わりは16世紀に遡るとしているほか<ref>{{Webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925133249/https://kingoryujin.org/about/ |date=2020年9月25日}}</ref>、大水口宿禰は、中世に消滅しかけた[[アラハバキ]]信仰の庇護者であるとしている<ref>{{Cite Web |url= https://kingoryujin.org/arahabaki/|title= アラハバキ神社／あらはばき神社｜東京・代々木・南新宿|publisher= 金吾龍神社|accessdate=2025-04-13}}</ref>。
=== 国造 === 『先代旧事本紀』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[末羅国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に穂積臣同祖の大水口足尼の孫の[[矢田稲吉命|矢田稲吉]]を国造に定める。のちの[[肥前国]][[松浦郡]]周辺にあたる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <ref name="keifu4">[[中田憲信]]「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> }}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大水口宿禰|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[大和神社]] （[[奈良県]][[天理市]]） * [[水口神社 (甲賀市)|水口神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]） * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=draft|12=draft}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=draft|12=draft}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=draft|12=draft}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=draft|12=draft}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=draft|12=draft}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|12=draft|12=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=draft|12=draft}}?

In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=draft|12=draft}} respectively. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|12=draft|12=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}

revision.comment: 50 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大水口宿禰 up to rev 104489856
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大水口宿禰命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大水口宿禰、大水口命、大水口足尼 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[孝霊天皇]]、[[崇神天皇]]、[[垂仁天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[采女氏|采女臣]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[出石心大臣命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>？、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]？、母：新河小楯姫<ref name="tenson"/>？ | 兄弟 = [[大矢口宿禰命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[建忍山垂根]]？ | 特記事項 = }} '''大水口宿禰'''（おおみなくちのすくね{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}/おおみくちのすくね）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の人物。
[[穂積氏|穂積臣（穂積氏）]]の遠祖とされる。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
== 系譜 == 系譜に関して『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京神別 天神 穂積臣」条では、[[伊香色雄|伊香賀色雄]]（伊我男命）と新河小楯姫命の子とも、[[ニギハヤヒ|神饒速日命]]の六世孫とも伝える。
一方、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天孫本紀」や「物部」系図では[[出石心大臣命]]（いずしこころのおおおみのみこと、饒速日尊三世孫）と新河小楯姫の子と記され、系譜に異同がある{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}<ref name="keifu4"/>。なお、同書では伊香色雄命（伊香賀色雄）は饒速日尊六世孫とされる。
子に関して史書では明らかでない。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]7年8月7日条によると、大水口宿禰は倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫（[[倭迹迹日百襲姫命]]）・[[伊勢麻績君]]（いせのおみのきみ）とともに同じ夢を見て、[[大物主|大物主神]]（のちの[[大神神社]]祭神）と[[倭大国魂神]]（のちの[[大和神社]]祭神）の祭主をそれぞれ[[大田田根子|大田田根子命]]と[[市磯長尾市]]にするよう告げられた旨を天皇に奏上した{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
また、同書[[垂仁天皇]]25年3月条では「一云」の中で、倭大神が大水口宿禰に神憑り、倭大神を祀ることを告げたことが記されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『日本書紀』では、大水口宿禰について[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]遠祖としている。
また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京神別 天神 [[穂積氏|穂積臣]] - 伊香賀色雄男の大水口宿禰の後。 * 右京神別 天神 [[采女氏|采女朝臣]] - 石上朝臣同祖。神饒速日命六世孫の大水口宿禰の後。
『先代旧事本紀』「天孫本紀」でも、同様に穂積臣・采女臣の祖である旨が記載されている{{Sfn|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
2013年より[[金吾龍神社]]の宮司を務める[[クリスチャントゥデイ#日本オリベットアッセンブリー教団（旧あいのひかり教団）に関する動向|水口結貴]]<ref>{{Cite Web|url=https://yuhobika.net/yuhobikaweb/kingoryu-jinja/|title=【金吾龍神社】古代祭祀の中心地に鎮座し 縄文信仰を今に伝える龍神神社 | ゆほびかweb|website=ゆほびかweb|publisher=マキノ出版|accessdate=2025-04-14}}</ref>が、大水口宿禰の後裔（88世大水口宿禰）を名乗っている。大水口宿禰と金吾龍神社との関わりは16世紀に遡るとしているほか<ref>{{Webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925133249/https://kingoryujin.org/about/ |date=2020年9月25日}}</ref>、大水口宿禰は、中世に消滅しかけた[[アラハバキ]]信仰の庇護者であるとしている<ref>{{Cite Web |url= https://kingoryujin.org/arahabaki/|title= アラハバキ神社／あらはばき神社｜東京・代々木・南新宿|publisher= 金吾龍神社|accessdate=2025-04-13}}</ref>。
=== 国造 === 『先代旧事本紀』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[末羅国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御世に穂積臣同祖の大水口足尼の孫の[[矢田稲吉命|矢田稲吉]]を国造に定める。のちの[[肥前国]][[松浦郡]]周辺にあたる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <ref name="keifu4">[[中田憲信]]「物部」『諸系譜』第四冊。</ref> }}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大水口宿禰|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大水口宿禰（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[穂積氏]] * [[大和神社]] （[[奈良県]][[天理市]]） * [[水口神社 (甲賀市)|水口神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[甲賀市]]） * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみなくちのすくね}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Yamato no Ōkunitama|ja|倭大国魂神}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oomizuguchi no Sukune}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Matsuura District, Nagasaki|ja|松浦郡|qq=draft}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōka|ja|甲賀市|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]]

Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|qq=}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]]

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}"Tenson Hongi" in [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|qq=}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[en:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese mythology figures]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|qq=}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|qq=}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:穂積氏]]

They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources.
