In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oomizuguchi no Sukune]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}}

Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|qq=|1=Takeru no Oyamadareneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041189}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}}

No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}}

No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|qq=|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}}

No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}}

They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Utsusikome|WD=Q11544859}}; {{ill|Q20041189|WD=Q20041189}}|father={{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|WD=Q55536799}}; {{ill|Q106106814|WD=Q106106814}}|relatives={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11436785]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|1=Uneme Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038916}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}"Tenson Hongi" in [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|1=Takeru no Oyamadareneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041189}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|1=Takeru no Oyamadareneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041189}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - They relayed to the emperor that [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato no Ōkunitama]] should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] and [[:en:Matsura-gun|Matsuura District, Nagasaki]]).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ([[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōka, Shiga|Kōka]]) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

His name does not appear in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].'' == Lineage == The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. In contrast, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato no Ōkunitama}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} and {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|WD=Q5973791|lt=Matsuura District, Nagasaki}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|1=Takeru no Oyamadareneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041189}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato no Ōkunitama}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} and {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|WD=Q5973791|lt=Matsuura District, Nagasaki}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Minakuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Q11548383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Empress Utsusikome|WD=Q11544859|1=Utsushikome}}; {{ill|Q20041189|WD=Q20041189|1=Keninoyama Tarine}}|father={{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|WD=Q55536799|1=Ikagashiko-o}}; {{ill|Q106106814|WD=Q106106814|1=Utsushiko'o-no-Mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938|1=Sakado no Yuratsuhime no Mikoto}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōminakuchi no Sukune | image = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Ōminakuchi no Sukune, Ōminakuchi no Mikoto, Ōminakuchi Ashinai | posthumous_name = | deified_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | sovereign = [[Emperor Kōrei]], [[Emperor Sujin]], [[Emperor Suinin]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|1=Uneme Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038916}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}"Tenson Hongi" in {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}.? or {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}?
Mother: Shinkawa Kodatehime? | siblings = {{ill|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|大矢口宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takesashinoyama Tatarine|ja|建忍山垂根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesashinoyama Tatarine|1=Keninoyama Tarine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041189}}? | notable_roles = Founder of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} }} '''Ōminakuchi no Sukune''' (大水口宿禰) is a figure in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, identified as an early ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. His name does not appear in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.'' == Lineage == The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''Kojiki'' do not provide explicit details about his lineage. However, the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' attributes his ancestry to {{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|ja|伊香賀色雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaga Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Shinkawa Kodatehime, or alternatively, as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}. In contrast, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamato-totohi-momoso-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato no Ōkunitama|1=Yamato Okunitama}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} and {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|WD=Q5973791|lt=Matsuura District, Nagasaki|1=Matsura-gun}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Q11548383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大水口宿禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11436785}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

In contrast, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' refers to him as a son of Izushi Kokoro no Ōmi no Mikoto and Shinkawa Kodatehime, with some discrepancies between sources. No historical texts explicitly identify his children. == Historical Records == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' on the 7th day of the 8th month of the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Ōminakuchi no Sukune, along with {{ill|Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja|倭迹速神浅茅原目妙姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatototohimomoso no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 倭迹迹日百襲姫命|1=Yamato-totohi-momoso-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387098}} and {{ill|Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja|伊勢麻績君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Omi no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had the same divine revelation in a dream. They relayed to the emperor that {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato no Ōkunitama|1=Yamato Okunitama}} should be enshrined by {{ill|Ōtataneko|ja|大田田根子|1=Ōtataneko|4=fr|5=Ōtataneko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtataneko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} and {{ill|Ichiiso Nagaoshi|ja|市磯長尾市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiso Nagaoshi|1=Ichishi no Nagaochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700600}} respectively. In the 25th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, an alternate account states that the deity of Yamato took possession of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, directing him to perform its worship. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''Nihon Shoki'' names Ōminakuchi no Sukune as a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}. The ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' also identifies the following clans as his descendants: * '''Sakyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Hozumi Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, son of Ikaga Shikoo. * '''Ukyō Shinbetsu Tenjin Uneme Clan''' - Descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune, sixth-generation descendant of Nigihayahi. The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' similarly identifies Ōminakuchi no Sukune as an ancestor of the Hozumi and Uneme clans. === Provincial Governors === The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' describes descendants of Ōminakuchi no Sukune serving as provincial governors: * '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' - During Emperor Seimu's reign, {{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a grandson of Ōminakuchi Ashinai, was appointed governor of the Matsura region (modern-day {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} and {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|WD=Q5973791|lt=Matsuura District, Nagasaki|1=Matsura-gun}}).''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280. == Notes == {{reflist|refs= [[Nakata Norinobu]] "Mononobe," ''Various Genealogies,'' Volume 4. }} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōminakuchi no Sukune|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary, Popular Edition|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}) * {{ill|Minakuchi Shrine|ja|水口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakuchi Shrine|1=Q11548383|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548383}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|WD=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}) * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{translated page|ja|大水口宿禰|version=104489856|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11436785|ja|大水口宿禰}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
